assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions).' The verdict is WARN, Eval Quality component is WARN due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions, and the combined Test Quality verdict is WARN as expected.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md 'Eval Result Detection' section states: 'An eval result review was detected from review ID 40001 via the 3-criteria heuristic: Author: github-actions[bot] (matches criterion 1 -- bot author), Marker: Body contains ## Eval Results (matches criterion 2), Footer: Body contains sdlc-workflow/run-evals (matches criterion 3).' The Eval Quality Summary table shows pass rates for all 5 evals (54/56 overall). Test Quality row reflects these eval metrics: 'eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate... overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions)'.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md classifies comment 50001 as 'code change request' from reviewer-b. The classification reasoning discusses the comment substantively as human review feedback ('The comment is from a human reviewer (reviewer-b, review ID 40002) who submitted a CHANGES_REQUESTED review') with no mention of eval detection, eval metrics, or eval processing. report.md Review Comment Classification table lists comment 50001 from reviewer-b as 'code change request' with action 'Sub-task created (subtask-1)', separately from the eval result note: 'Review 40001 from github-actions[bot] was correctly identified as an eval result review (not a human review comment)'.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN -- eval-3 has 2 failing assertions at 85% pass rate (convention upgrade eligibility not evaluated for comment 30002, no sub-task created for comment 30002); overall eval pass rate 91% (54/56 assertions).' The row explicitly names eval-3's two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation for comment 30002.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md is typed 'eval-failure' with title 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures: convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation'. It includes both failing assertions with full text and evidence: Assertion 1: 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with evidence about review-30002.md lacking convention upgrade analysis; Assertion 2: 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence about no sub-task being created.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md has a '## Review Context' section containing: 'Eval ID: eval-3', 'Pass Rate: 85% (11/13 passed, 2 failed)', followed by both failing assertions with full text and evidence. Failing Assertion 1 quotes 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002...' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility -- no CONVENTIONS.md lookup or codebase pattern analysis is documented in the classification reasoning'. Failing Assertion 2 quotes 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path...' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted, so the suggestion was not elevated to a code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-2.md (eval-failure) has '## Target PR' section with 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-1.md (review-feedback) also has '## Target PR' with the same URL. These are the actionable sub-tasks whose changes should be committed to the existing PR branch. Root-cause sub-tasks (subtask-3, subtask-4) do not have Target PR sections, which is expected since root-cause tasks create separate branches for convention/skill fixes.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | 2 root-cause tasks created: convention gap for excluding repository’s primary file type (subtask-3), implement-task skill gap for convention upgrade documentation requirement (subtask-4) |'. The verdict is 'DONE' (not N/A). subtask-3.md addresses the convention gap (type: root-cause) and subtask-4.md addresses the skill gap for convention upgrade documentation (type: root-cause). subtask-4 specifically targets eval-3's failures: 'The eval-3 assertion failures reveal that the verify-pr skill’s implementation does not always evaluate convention upgrade eligibility for suggestion-classified comments.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 14 files in the outputs directory are verification artifacts: report.md (verification report), review-50001.md (review classification), criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md (acceptance criteria evaluations), subtask-1.md through subtask-4.md (sub-task descriptions). None are code files. The sub-task description files describe future work to be done (Files to Modify sections list files but do not contain code changes). No source code files (.ts, .js, .py, or plugin skill files) appear in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Scope Containment: '| Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task: style-conventions.md and SKILL.md |' — compares diff files against task spec with no eval reference. Sensitive Patterns: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in the diff |' — scans diff for secrets with no eval reference. Acceptance Criteria: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 7/7 criteria met -- Check 6 scans for new symbols (criterion 1)...' — evaluates criteria against diff content. All 7 criterion files (criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md) evaluate PR diff content against specific acceptance criteria with no references to eval results or eval processing.
